steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the overall structure of the passage. Map out what the passage actually does: (1) introduces intermittent mass loss in asteroid 6478 Gault, (2) rules out solar energy-driven ice vaporization ('not attributable'), (3) rules out impact ejection ('untenable'), and (4) presents rotational instability as the likely correct explanation.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'introduces a natural phenomenon, refutes two potential explanations, and presents a third as plausible': matches the passage exactly — phenomenon (mass loss), refutations (solar energy, impact ejection), third explanation (rotational instability, 'likely correct'). ✓
  • Choice B — 'describes a finding, discusses flawed theories, then describes new evidence supporting an alternative': the passage never presents new evidence; it cites Luu et al.'s reasoning, not fresh data. ✗
  • Choice C — 'evaluates causes and states no persuasive account has been put forward': the opposite is true — the passage ends by endorsing rotational instability as likely correct. ✗
  • Choice D — 'presents an observation, contrasts it with other observations, then explains why those others are not credible': the passage doesn't discredit other asteroid observations — it rules out two explanations for this asteroid's mass loss. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
